availableCommands.name: debug
availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues (bundled)

availableCommands.name: simplify
availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: batch
availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: loop
availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) (bundled)

availableCommands.name: claude-api
availableCommands.description:
Build apps with the Claude API or Anthropic SDK.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`/`claude_agent_sdk`, or user asks to use Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Agent SDK.
DO NOT TRIGGER when: code imports `openai`/other AI SDK, general programming, or ML/data-science tasks. (bundled)

availableCommands.name: gsd:settings
availableCommands.description: Configure GSD workflow toggles and model profile (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:audit-milestone
availableCommands.description: Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:research-phase
availableCommands.description: Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gsd:plan-phase instead) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:discuss-phase
availableCommands.description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:remove-phase
availableCommands.description: Remove a future phase from roadmap and renumber subsequent phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-phase
availableCommands.description: Add phase to end of current milestone in roadmap (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:quick
availableCommands.description: Execute a quick task with GSD guarantees (atomic commits, state tracking) but skip optional agents (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:cleanup
availableCommands.description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:health
availableCommands.description: Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:reapply-patches
availableCommands.description: Reapply local modifications after a GSD update (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:complete-milestone
availableCommands.description: Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:pause-work
availableCommands.description: Create context handoff when pausing work mid-phase (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:check-todos
availableCommands.description: List pending todos and select one to work on (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:list-phase-assumptions
availableCommands.description: Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-milestone
availableCommands.description: Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:progress
availableCommands.description: Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:resume-work
availableCommands.description: Resume work from previous session with full context restoration (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:execute-phase
availableCommands.description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:new-project
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:set-profile
availableCommands.description: Switch model profile for GSD agents (quality/balanced/budget) (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:map-codebase
availableCommands.description: Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents (user)

input.hint: [optional: specific area to map, e.g., 'api' or 'auth']

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-milestone-gaps
availableCommands.description: Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:verify-work
availableCommands.description: Validate built features through conversational UAT (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:debug
availableCommands.description: Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:update
availableCommands.description: Update GSD to latest version with changelog display (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:help
availableCommands.description: Show available GSD commands and usage guide (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:insert-phase
availableCommands.description: Insert urgent work as decimal phase (e.g., 72.1) between existing phases (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:join-discord
availableCommands.description: Join the GSD Discord community (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:add-todo
availableCommands.description: Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context (user)

availableCommands.name: gsd:plan-phase
availableCommands.description: Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop (user)

availableCommands.name: compact
availableCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

availableCommands.name: context
availableCommands.description: Show current context usage

availableCommands.name: heapdump
availableCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

availableCommands.name: init
availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

availableCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

1→import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'fs'
     2→import { readdir, readFile, stat } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join, isAbsolute } from 'path'
     4→import { homedir } from 'os'
     5→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
     6→const Database = require('better-sqlite3')
     7→
     8→export interface DiscoveredSession {
     9→  sessionId: string
    10→  slug: string
    11→  lastModified: number
    12→  cwd: string
    13→  fileSize: number
    14→}
    15→
    16→export type SessionBackend = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    17→
    18→// Message types that indicate actual conversation content (not just summaries)
    19→const CONVERSATION_TYPES = ['user', 'assistant']
    20→const OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME = '.opencode.json'
    21→
    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23→  // Claude encodes paths by:
    24→  // 1. Removing trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replacing / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replacing _ with -
    27→  // 4. Replacing spaces with -
    28→  // 5. Replacing : with - (Windows drive letters)
    29→  // 6. Replacing dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  // /home/user/my_project/ becomes -home-user-my-project
    31→  // /home/user/.config/app becomes -home-user--config-app
    32→  // C:\Users\bob\project becomes -C-Users-bob-project
    33→  return projectPath
    34→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    35→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    36→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    37→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    38→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    39→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    40→}
    41→
    42→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    43→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    44→    return homedir()
    45→  }
    46→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    47→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    48→  }
    49→  return targetPath
    50→}
    51→
    52→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    53→  const home = homedir()
    54→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    55→  const candidatePaths = [
    56→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    57→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    58→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    59→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    60→  ]
    61→
    62→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    63→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    64→    try {
    65→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    66→      return JSON.parse(content)
    67→    } catch (e) {
    68→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    69→    }
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  return null
    73→}
    74→
    75→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    76→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
    77→  const configured = config?.data?.directory?.trim()
    78→  const dataDir = configured && configured.length > 0 ? configured : '.opencode'
    79→  const expanded = expandHomePath(dataDir)
    80→  return isAbsolute(expanded) ? expanded : join(projectPath, expanded)
    81→}
    82→
    83→async function discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath: string): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
    84→  const claudeDir = join(homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')
    85→  const encodedPath = encodeProjectPath(projectPath)
    86→  const projectSessionsDir = join(claudeDir, encodedPath)
    87→
    88→  if (!existsSync(projectSessionsDir)) {
    89→    return []
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  const sessions: DiscoveredSession[] = []
    93→
    94→  try {
    95→    const files = await readdir(projectSessionsDir)
    96→
    97→    // Process files in parallel for better performance
    98→    const results = await Promise.all(
    99→      files
   100→        .filter(file => file.endsWith('.jsonl') && !file.startsWith('agent-'))
   101→        .map(async (file) => {
   102→          const sessionId = file.replace('.jsonl', '')
   103→          const filePath = join(projectSessionsDir, file)
   104→
   105→          try {
   106→            const fileStat = await stat(filePath)
   107→            const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
   108→            const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())
   109→
   110→            // Look for slug and check if session has actual conversation
   111→            let slug = sessionId.slice(0, 8)
   112→            let cwd = projectPath
   113→            let hasConversation = false
   114→
   115→            for (const line of lines) {
   116→              try {
   117→                const data = JSON.parse(line)
   118→                if (data.slug) slug = data.slug
   119→                if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
   120→                if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {
   121→                  hasConversation = true
   122→                }
   123→              } catch {
   124→                // Skip non-JSON lines
   125→              }
   126→            }
   127→
   128→            if (!hasConversation) {
   129→              return null
   130→            }
   131→
   132→            return {
   133→              sessionId,
   134→              slug,
   135→              lastModified: fileStat.mtimeMs,
   136→              cwd,
   137→              fileSize: fileStat.size
   138→            }
   139→          } catch (e) {
   140→            console.error(`Failed to parse session ${file}:`, e)
   141→            return null
   142→          }
   143→        })
   144→    )
   145→
   146→    // Filter out nulls and add to sessions
   147→    for (const result of results) {
   148→      if (result) sessions.push(result)
   149→    }
   150→  } catch (e) {
   151→    console.error('Failed to read sessions directory:', e)
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  // Sort by most recent first
   155→  sessions.sort((a, b) => b.lastModified - a.lastModified)
   156→
   157→  return sessions
   158→}
   159→
   160→function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {
   161→  const dataDir = resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath)
   162→  const dbPath = join(dataDir, 'opencode.db')
   163→
   164→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
   165→    return []
   166→  }
   167→
   168→  try {
   169→    const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
   170→    const rows = db.prepare(`
   171→      SELECT id, title, updated_at, created_at, message_count
   172→      FROM sessions
   173→      WHERE parent_session_id IS NULL
   174→      ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   175→    `).all() as Array<{ id: string; title: string | null; updated_at: number; created_at: number; message_count: number }>
   176→    db.close()
   177→
   178→    return rows
   179→      .filter(row => (row.message_count ?? 0) > 0)
   180→      .map(row => {
   181→        const lastModifiedSeconds = row.updated_at || row.created_at || 0
   182→        return {
   183→          sessionId: row.id,
   184→          slug: (row.title && row.title.trim()) || row.id.slice(0, 8),
   185→          lastModified: Number(lastModifiedSeconds) * 1000,
   186→          cwd: projectPath,
   187→          fileSize: 0
   188→        }
   189→      })
   190→  } catch (e) {
   191→    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
   192→    return []
   193→  }
   194→}
   195→
   196→export async function discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' = 'claude'): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
   197→  if (backend === 'opencode') {
   198→    return discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath)
   199→  }
   200→  return discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath)
   201→}
   202→

   181→        const lastModifiedSeconds = row.updated_at || row.created_at || 0
   182→        return {
   183→          sessionId: row.id,
   184→          slug: (row.title && row.title.trim()) || row.id.slice(0, 8),
   185→          lastModified: Number(lastModifiedSeconds) * 1000,
   186→          cwd: projectPath,
   187→          fileSize: 0
   188→        }
   189→      })
   190→  } catch (e) {
   191→    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
   192→    return []
   193→  }
   194→}
   195→
   196→export async function discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' = 'claude'): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
   197→  if (backend === 'opencode') {
   198→    return discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath)
   199→  }
   200→  return discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath)
   201→}
   202→
```

     1→import { app } from 'electron'

     3→import { join } from 'path'

     5→
     6→export interface ProjectCategory {

    17→  apiPort?: number  // Port for HTTP API to send prompts to terminal
    18→  apiAutoStart?: boolean  // Whether to auto-start API when session opens (default: false)
    19→  apiSessionMode?: 'existing' | 'new-keep' | 'new-close'  // How API requests handle sessions
    20→  apiModel?: 'default' | 'opus' | 'sonnet' | 'haiku'  // Model for API-triggered sessions
    21→  autoAcceptTools?: string[]  // Per-project tool patterns to auto-accept
    22→  permissionMode?: string     // Per-project permission mode

    27→  categoryId?: string         // Category this project belongs to

    29→}
    30→

    94→}
    95→
    96→
    97→interface StoredData {
    98→  workspace: Workspace
    99→  windowBounds?: WindowBounds
   100→  settings?: Settings
   101→}
   102→
   103→export class SessionStore {
   104→  private configPath: string
   105→  private backupPath: string
   106→  private data: StoredData
   107→
   108→  constructor() {
   109→    const configDir = join(app.getPath('userData'), 'config')
   110→    if (!existsSync(configDir)) {
   111→      mkdirSync(configDir, { recursive: true })
   112→    }
   113→    this.configPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json')
   114→    this.backupPath = join(configDir, 'workspace.json.backup')
   115→    this.data = this.load()
   116→  }
   117→
   118→  private loadFile(path: string): StoredData | null {
   119→    try {
   120→      if (!existsSync(path)) return null
   121→      const stat = statSync(path)
   122→      if (stat.size === 0) return null
   123→      const content = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
   124→      const data = JSON.parse(content)
   125→      if (!data?.workspace) return null
   126→      return data
   127→    } catch (e) {
   128→      console.error(`[SessionStore] Failed to load ${path}:`, e)
   129→      return null
   130→    }
   131→  }
   132→
   133→  private load(): StoredData {
   134→    // Try main file first
   135→    const main = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   136→    if (main && (main.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   137→      return main
   138→    }
   139→
   140→    // Main file is empty/corrupt/missing — try backup
   141→    const backup = this.loadFile(this.backupPath)
   142→    if (backup && (backup.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   143→      console.log('[SessionStore] Main config empty/corrupt, restored from backup (' +
   144→        backup.workspace.projects.length + ' projects)')
   145→      // Restore backup as main file immediately
   146→      try {
   147→        writeFileSync(this.configPath, JSON.stringify(backup, null, 2))
   148→      } catch (e) {
   149→        console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to restore backup to main:', e)
   150→      }
   151→      return backup
   152→    }
   153→
   154→    // If main loaded but had 0 projects (valid empty state), use it
   155→    if (main) return main
   156→
   157→    return {
   158→      workspace: {
   159→        projects: [],
   160→        openTabs: [],
   161→        activeTabId: null
   162→      }
   163→    }
   164→  }
   165→
   166→  private save(): void {
   167→    try {
   168→      const json = JSON.stringify(this.data, null, 2)
   169→
   170→      // Validate we can parse what we're about to write
   171→      JSON.parse(json)
   172→
   173→      // Back up existing file before overwriting (if it has content)
   174→      if (existsSync(this.configPath)) {
   175→        try {
   176→          const stat = statSync(this.configPath)
   177→          if (stat.size > 0) {
   178→            // Only update backup if existing file is valid and has projects
   179→            const existing = this.loadFile(this.configPath)
   180→            if (existing && (existing.workspace.projects?.length || 0) > 0) {
   181→              writeFileSync(this.backupPath, readFileSync(this.configPath))
   182→            }
   183→          }
   184→        } catch (e) {
   185→          console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to update backup:', e)
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→
   189→      // Atomic write: write to temp file, then rename
   190→      const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
   191→      writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
   192→
   193→      // Verify the temp file was written completely
   194→      const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
   195→      JSON.parse(written) // throws if truncated/corrupt
   196→
   197→      renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
   198→    } catch (e) {
   199→      console.error('[SessionStore] Failed to save workspace:', e)
   200→    }
   201→  }
   202→
   203→  getWorkspace(): Workspace {
   204→    return this.data.workspace
   205→  }
   206→
   207→  // Reload workspace from disk (useful when file was modified externally)
   208→  reloadFromDisk(): void {
   209→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloading workspace from disk')
   210→    this.data = this.load()
   211→    console.log('[SessionStore] Reloaded, projects:', this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0)
   212→  }
   213→
   214→  saveWorkspace(workspace: Workspace): void {
   215→    // Protect against overwriting populated workspace with empty one
   216→    const incomingProjects = workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   217→    const currentProjects = this.data.workspace?.projects?.length || 0
   218→    if (incomingProjects === 0 && currentProjects > 0) {
   219→      console.log('[SessionStore] Rejected empty workspace save - current has', currentProjects, 'projects')
   220→      return
   221→    }
   222→    this.data.workspace = workspace
   223→    this.save()
   224→    syncMetaProjects(workspace)
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  getWindowBounds(): WindowBounds | undefined {
   228→    return this.data.windowBounds
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  saveWindowBounds(bounds: WindowBounds): void {
   232→    this.data.windowBounds = bounds
   233→    this.save()
   234→  }
   235→
   236→  getSettings(): Settings {
   237→    return this.data.settings ?? { defaultProjectDir: '', theme: 'default', backend: 'default' }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  saveSettings(settings: Settings): void {
   241→    this.data.settings = settings
   242→    this.save()
   243→  }
   244→}
   245→
```

     1→/**
     2→ * Kspec IPC Handlers
     3→ *
     4→ * Minimal handlers for operations that need main process access:
     5→ * - Filesystem checks (.kspec/ existence)
     6→ * - Project initialization (runs kspec CLI)
     7→ * - Daemon lifecycle (start/stop)
     8→ *
     9→ * Most kspec operations go directly to the daemon HTTP API from the renderer.
    10→ * These handlers only cover what can't be done from the browser context.

1→import * as pty from 'node-pty'
     2→import * as fs from 'fs'
     3→import * as path from 'path'
     4→import {
     5→  isWindows,
     6→  getEnhancedPathWithPortable,
     7→  getAdditionalPaths,
     8→} from './platform'
     9→import { getPortableBinDirs } from './portable-deps'
    10→
    11→// Backends that use Ink (React for CLI) and are sensitive to rapid resize during startup
    12→const INK_BACKENDS = new Set(['gemini'])
    13→
    14→// Ring buffer for storing recent PTY output
    15→const OUTPUT_BUFFER_MAX_LINES = 200
    16→
    17→class OutputBuffer {
    18→  private lines: string[] = []
    19→  private partial: string = '' // incomplete line (no newline yet)
    20→
    21→  append(data: string): void {
    22→    // Split incoming data by newlines, preserving partial lines
    23→    const text = this.partial + data
    24→    const parts = text.split('\n')
    25→    // Last element is either empty (if data ended with \n) or a partial line
    26→    this.partial = parts.pop() || ''
    27→    for (const line of parts) {
    28→      // Strip ANSI escape sequences for readability
    29→      const clean = line.replace(/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g, '').replace(/\x1b\][^\x07]*\x07/g, '').trim()
    30→      if (clean) {
    31→        this.lines.push(clean)
    32→        if (this.lines.length > OUTPUT_BUFFER_MAX_LINES) {
    33→          this.lines.shift()
    34→        }
    35→      }
    36→    }
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  getRecent(maxLines: number = 50): string[] {
    40→    return this.lines.slice(-maxLines)
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  clear(): void {
    44→    this.lines = []
    45→    this.partial = ''
    46→  }
    47→}
    48→
    49→interface ClaudeProcess {
    50→  id: string
    51→  pty: pty.IPty
    52→  cwd: string
    53→  sessionId?: string
    54→  backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    55→  disposables: { dispose: () => void }[]
    56→  spawnedAt: number
    57→  resizeTimeout?: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>
    58→  lastResizeCols?: number
    59→  lastResizeRows?: number
    60→  outputBuffer: OutputBuffer
    61→}
    62→
    63→// Extended node-pty options - useConpty is a Windows-specific option not in @types/node-pty
    64→interface ExtendedPtyForkOptions extends pty.IPtyForkOptions {
    65→  useConpty?: boolean
    66→}
    67→

     1→import * as pty from 'node-pty'
     2→import * as fs from 'fs'
     3→import * as path from 'path'
     4→import {
     5→  isWindows,
     6→  getEnhancedPathWithPortable,
     7→  getAdditionalPaths,
     8→} from './platform'
     9→import { getPortableBinDirs } from './portable-deps'
    10→
    11→// Backends that use Ink (React for CLI) and are sensitive to rapid resize during startup
    12→const INK_BACKENDS = new Set(['gemini'])
    13→
    14→// Ring buffer for storing recent PTY output
    15→const OUTPUT_BUFFER_MAX_LINES = 200
    16→
    17→class OutputBuffer {
    18→  private lines: string[] = []
    19→  private partial: string = '' // incomplete line (no newline yet)
    20→
    21→  append(data: string): void {
    22→    // Split incoming data by newlines, preserving partial lines
    23→    const text = this.partial + data
    24→    const parts = text.split('\n')
    25→    // Last element is either empty (if data ended with \n) or a partial line
    26→    this.partial = parts.pop() || ''
    27→    for (const line of parts) {
    28→      // Strip ANSI escape sequences for readability
    29→      const clean = line.replace(/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]/g, '').replace(/\x1b\][^\x07]*\x07/g, '').trim()
    30→      if (clean) {
    31→        this.lines.push(clean)
    32→        if (this.lines.length > OUTPUT_BUFFER_MAX_LINES) {
    33→          this.lines.shift()
    34→        }
    35→      }
    36→    }
    37→  }
    38→
    39→  getRecent(maxLines: number = 50): string[] {
    40→    return this.lines.slice(-maxLines)
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  clear(): void {
    44→    this.lines = []
    45→    this.partial = ''
    46→  }
    47→}
    48→
    49→interface ClaudeProcess {
    50→  id: string
    51→  pty: pty.IPty
    52→  cwd: string
    53→  sessionId?: string
    54→  backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    55→  disposables: { dispose: () => void }[]
    56→  spawnedAt: number
    57→  resizeTimeout?: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>
    58→  lastResizeCols?: number
    59→  lastResizeRows?: number
    60→  outputBuffer: OutputBuffer
    61→}
    62→
    63→// Extended node-pty options - useConpty is a Windows-specific option not in @types/node-pty
    64→interface ExtendedPtyForkOptions extends pty.IPtyForkOptions {
    65→  useConpty?: boolean
    66→}
    67→
    68→function getEnhancedEnv(): { [key: string]: string } {
    69→  const env = { ...process.env } as { [key: string]: string }
    70→  delete env.CLAUDECODE
    71→  const enhancedPath = getEnhancedPathWithPortable()
    72→
    73→  // On Windows, environment variables are case-insensitive but we need to set the right one
    74→  if (isWindows) {
    75→    // Windows uses 'Path' but Node sometimes uses 'PATH' - set both to be safe
    76→    env.PATH = enhancedPath
    77→    env.Path = enhancedPath
    78→
    79→    // Claude Code on Windows requires git-bash - try to find and set it
    80→    if (!env.CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_BASH_PATH) {
    81→      const gitBashPaths = [
    82→        'C:\\Program Files\\Git\\bin\\bash.exe',
    83→        'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Git\\bin\\bash.exe',
    84→        path.join(
    85→          process.env.LOCALAPPDATA || '',
    86→          'Programs',
    87→          'Git',
    88→          'bin',
    89→          'bash.exe'
    90→        ),
    91→        path.join(process.env.PROGRAMFILES || '', 'Git', 'bin', 'bash.exe'),
    92→      ]
    93→      for (const bashPath of gitBashPaths) {
    94→        if (fs.existsSync(bashPath)) {
    95→          env.CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_BASH_PATH = bashPath
    96→          console.log('Found git-bash at:', bashPath)
    97→          break
    98→        }
    99→      }
   100→    }

     2→ * Terminal Routes
     3→ *
     4→ * HTTP endpoints for terminal/PTY management.
     5→ * WebSocket is used for real-time data streaming (see ws-handler.ts).
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import { Router, Request, Response } from 'express'
     9→import {
    10→  ApiResponse,
    11→  TerminalCreateRequest,
    12→  TerminalCreateResponse,
    13→  TerminalSession,
    14→  Backend
    15→} from '../types'
    16→import { getServices } from '../app'
    17→
    18→const router = Router()
    19→
    20→// =============================================================================
    21→// In-Memory Terminal Session Tracking
    22→// =============================================================================
    23→
    24→// Track active terminal sessions created via this API
    25→const activeSessions: Map<string, TerminalSession> = new Map()
    26→
    27→// =============================================================================
    28→// Helper Functions
    29→// =============================================================================
    30→
    31→function sendResponse<T>(res: Response, statusCode: number, data: ApiResponse<T>): void {
    32→  res.status(statusCode).json(data)
    33→}
    34→
    35→function sendError(res: Response, statusCode: number, error: string): void {
    36→  sendResponse(res, statusCode, {
    37→    success: false,
    38→    error,
    39→    timestamp: Date.now()
    40→  })
    41→}
    42→
    43→// =============================================================================
    44→// Routes
    45→// =============================================================================
    46→
    47→/**
    48→ * POST /api/terminal/create
    49→ * Create a new terminal session
    50→ *
    51→ * Body: { projectPath: string, sessionId?: string, model?: string, backend?: Backend }
    52→ * Returns: { ptyId: string, projectPath: string, backend?: Backend }
    53→ */
    54→router.post('/create', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
    55→  try {
    56→    const services = getServices()
    57→
    58→    if (!services.spawnPty) {
    59→      return sendError(res, 503, 'Terminal service not available')
    60→    }
    61→
    62→    const body: TerminalCreateRequest = req.body
    63→
    64→    if (!body.projectPath) {
    65→      return sendError(res, 400, 'projectPath is required')
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    // Validate backend if provided
    69→    const validBackends: Backend[] = ['claude', 'gemini', 'codex', 'opencode', 'aider']
    70→    if (body.backend && !validBackends.includes(body.backend)) {
    71→      return sendError(res, 400, `Invalid backend. Must be one of: ${validBackends.join(', ')}`)
    72→    }
    73→
    74→    // Spawn PTY process
    75→    const ptyId = await services.spawnPty(
    76→      body.projectPath,
    77→      body.sessionId,
    78→      body.model,
    79→      body.backend
    80→    )
    81→
    82→    // Track the session
    83→    const session: TerminalSession = {
    84→      ptyId,
    85→      projectPath: body.projectPath,
    86→      sessionId: body.sessionId,
    87→      backend: body.backend,
    88→      createdAt: Date.now()
    89→    }
    90→    activeSessions.set(ptyId, session)
    91→
    92→    const response: ApiResponse<TerminalCreateResponse> = {
    93→      success: true,
    94→      data: {
    95→        ptyId,
    96→        projectPath: body.projectPath,
    97→        backend: body.backend
    98→      },
    99→      timestamp: Date.now()
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    sendResponse(res, 201, response)
   103→  } catch (error: any) {
   104→    console.error('[Terminal Route] Create error:', error)
   105→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to create terminal')
   106→  }
   107→})
   108→
   109→/**
   110→ * GET /api/terminal/sessions
   111→ * List all active terminal sessions
   112→ *
   113→ * Returns: TerminalSession[]

   117→    const sessions = Array.from(activeSessions.values())
   118→

   120→      success: true,
   121→      data: sessions,
   122→      timestamp: Date.now()
   123→    })

   125→    sendError(res, 500, error.message || 'Failed to list sessions')
   126→  }

     2→import { join } from 'path'

     8→export function registerWorkspaceHandlers(
     9→  sessionStore: SessionStore,
    10→  getMainWindow: () => BrowserWindow | null
    11→): void {
    12→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:get', () => sessionStore.getWorkspace())
    13→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:save', (_, workspace) => sessionStore.saveWorkspace(workspace))
    14→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:getMetaProjectsPath', () => getMetaProjectsPath())
    15→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:getCategoryMetaPath', (_, categoryName: string) => {
    16→    const basePath = getMetaProjectsPath()
    17→    // Sanitize category name for filesystem
    18→    const safeName = categoryName.replace(/[/\\:*?"<>|]/g, '_')
    19→    return join(basePath, safeName)
    20→  })
    21→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:addProject', async () => {
    22→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    23→    const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    24→      properties: ['openDirectory'],
    25→      title: 'Select Project Folder'
    26→    })
    27→    return result.canceled ? null : result.filePaths[0] || null
    28→  })
    29→
    30→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:addProjectsFromParent', async () => {
    31→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    32→    const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    33→      properties: ['openDirectory'],
    34→      title: 'Select Parent Folder (all subdirectories will be added as projects)'
    35→    })
    36→    if (result.canceled || !result.filePaths[0]) return null
    37→
    38→    const parentDir = result.filePaths[0]
    39→
    40→    try {
    41→      const entries = readdirSync(parentDir, { withFileTypes: true })
    42→      const subdirs = entries
    43→        .filter((entry) => entry.isDirectory() && !entry.name.startsWith('.'))
    44→        .map((entry) => ({
    45→          path: join(parentDir, entry.name),
    46→          name: entry.name
    47→        }))
    48→      return subdirs
    49→    } catch (e) {
    50→      console.error('Failed to scan parent directory:', e)
    51→      return null
    52→    }
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  // Session discovery
    56→  ipcMain.handle('sessions:discover', (_, projectPath: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => discoverSessions(projectPath, backend))
    57→}
    58→

```
     1→import { BrowserWindow, dialog, ipcMain } from 'electron'
     2→import { join } from 'path'
     3→import { readdirSync, statSync } from 'fs'
     4→import { SessionStore } from '../../session-store.js'
     5→import { getMetaProjectsPath } from '../../meta-project-sync.js'
     6→import { discoverSessions } from '../../session-discovery.js'
     7→
     8→export function registerWorkspaceHandlers(
     9→  sessionStore: SessionStore,
    10→  getMainWindow: () => BrowserWindow | null
    11→): void {
    12→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:get', () => sessionStore.getWorkspace())
    13→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:save', (_, workspace) => sessionStore.saveWorkspace(workspace))
    14→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:getMetaProjectsPath', () => getMetaProjectsPath())
    15→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:getCategoryMetaPath', (_, categoryName: string) => {
    16→    const basePath = getMetaProjectsPath()
    17→    // Sanitize category name for filesystem
    18→    const safeName = categoryName.replace(/[/\\:*?"<>|]/g, '_')
    19→    return join(basePath, safeName)
    20→  })
    21→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:addProject', async () => {
    22→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    23→    const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    24→      properties: ['openDirectory'],
    25→      title: 'Select Project Folder'
    26→    })
    27→    return result.canceled ? null : result.filePaths[0] || null
    28→  })
    29→
    30→  ipcMain.handle('workspace:addProjectsFromParent', async () => {
    31→    const mainWindow = getMainWindow()
    32→    const result = await dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow!, {
    33→      properties: ['openDirectory'],
    34→      title: 'Select Parent Folder (all subdirectories will be added as projects)'
    35→    })
    36→    if (result.canceled || !result.filePaths[0]) return null
    37→
    38→    const parentDir = result.filePaths[0]
    39→
    40→    try {
    41→      const entries = readdirSync(parentDir, { withFileTypes: true })
    42→      const subdirs = entries
    43→        .filter((entry) => entry.isDirectory() && !entry.name.startsWith('.'))
    44→        .map((entry) => ({
    45→          path: join(parentDir, entry.name),
    46→          name: entry.name
    47→        }))
    48→      return subdirs
    49→    } catch (e) {
    50→      console.error('Failed to scan parent directory:', e)
    51→      return null
    52→    }
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  // Session discovery
    56→  ipcMain.handle('sessions:discover', (_, projectPath: string, backend?: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider') => discoverSessions(projectPath, backend))
    57→}
    58→
```

commit 758a5a91e1520a3748b483033c94b0f7ba14af19
Author: DonutsDelivery <megusta52@proton.me>
Date:   Tue Mar 10 05:54:49 2026 +0100

    fix: workspace persistence bugs — atomic writes, temp cleanup, mobile route, stale symlinks
    
    1. session-store: clean up temp file on verification failure (was leaked on error)
    2. session-store: use atomic temp+rename for backup write and restore (was non-atomic)
    3. mobile-server POST /api/workspace: fix wrong property paths (current.workspace.projects
       → current.projects) and unwrap body before passing to saveWorkspace
    4. meta-project-sync: detect and remove stale symlinks pointing to deleted directories

    Spec: @workspace-persist
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

_ulid: 01KK67BAXYFC28NYYSCWDPARHC
slugs:
  - terminal
title: Terminal Management
type: module
status:
  maturity: draft
  implementation: not_started
description: Core PTY session management, multi-backend CLI support, and terminal lifecycle
tags:
  - core
depends_on: []
implements: []
relates_to: []
tests: []
traits: []
notes: []
features:
  - _ulid: 01KK67BPRJVNKGYEGMA7EPB4TY
    slugs:
      - pty-lifecycle
    title: PTY Session Lifecycle
    type: feature
    tags: []
    description: Spawn, manage, resize, and terminate PTY processes running AI CLI backends. Ring buffer
      captures last 200 lines with ANSI stripping.
    depends_on: []
    implements: []
    relates_to: []
    tests: []
    traits: []
    notes: []
    created: 2026-03-08T08:00:01.811Z
    acceptance_criteria:
      - id: ac-1
        given: user opens a project
        when: a terminal session is spawned
        then: a PTY process is created with node-pty using the resolved backend executable, correct cwd, and
          xterm-256color terminal type
      - id: ac-2
        given: a PTY session is running
        when: terminal output is produced
        then: output is captured in a 200-line ring buffer with ANSI codes stripped and relayed to renderer
          via IPC
      - id: ac-3
        given: a PTY session is running
        when: the user closes the tab or app exits
        then: the PTY process is terminated and all listeners are cleaned up
    status:
      maturity: draft
      implementation: implemented
  - _ulid: 01KK67BRJR4864JXD28BE8HNK2
    slugs:
      - multi-backend
    title: Multi-Backend Support
    type: feature
    tags: []
    description: Support multiple AI CLI backends (Claude, Gemini, Codex, OpenCode, Aider) with
      backend-specific argument mapping for permissions, session resumption, and tool acceptance.
    depends_on: []
    implements: []
    relates_to: []
    tests: []
    traits: []
    notes: []
    created: 2026-03-08T08:00:03.672Z
    acceptance_criteria:
      - id: ac-1
        given: a project has backend set to gemini
        when: a session is spawned
        then: the gemini CLI executable is located and launched with Gemini-specific flags for approval mode
          and allowed tools
      - id: ac-2
        given: a backend has permission mode configured
        when: a session is spawned
        then: "backend-specific CLI flags are applied: Claude uses --permission-mode, Gemini uses
          --approval-mode, Codex uses --full-auto, Aider uses --yes"
    status:
      implementation: implemented
  - _ulid: 01KK67BTQMCZXZ3WPE6KE4K8FF
    slugs:
      - session-discovery
    title: Session Discovery
    type: feature
    tags: []
    description: Discover previous sessions from Claude (~/.claude/projects/), OpenCode
      (.opencode/opencode.db via SQLite), and other backends to enable session resumption.
    depends_on: []
    implements: []
    relates_to: []
    tests: []
    traits: []
    notes: []
    created: 2026-03-08T08:00:05.876Z
    status:
      maturity: draft
      implementation: in_progress
  - _ulid: 01KK67BWF9SHA4CYVW2K38CWKK
    slugs:
      - backend-switching
    title: Runtime Backend Switching
    type: feature
    tags: []
    description: Switch the AI backend of a running terminal session without closing the tile. Kills old
      PTY, spawns new one with same cwd, preserves session ID when backends match.
    depends_on: []
    implements: []
    relates_to: []
    tests: []
    traits: []
    notes: []
    created: 2026-03-08T08:00:07.658Z
    status:
      implementation: implemented
  - _ulid: 01KK67BXW9RQP8D62KHVSF3CW9
    slugs:
      - cli-install
    title: CLI Tool Installation
    type: feature
    tags: []
    description: Check, install, and manage AI CLI tools (Claude, Gemini, Codex, OpenCode, Aider) with
      portable Node.js/Python support for offline-first workflows.
    depends_on: []
    implements: []
    relates_to: []
    tests: []
    traits: []
    notes: []
    created: 2026-03-08T08:00:09.097Z
    status:
      implementation: implemented
constraints:

    slugs:
      - context-bridge
    title: Context Bridge Isolation
    type: constraint
    tags: []
    description: All main process capabilities exposed to renderer through Electron contextBridge. No

    depends_on: []
    implements: []
    relates_to: []
    tests: []
    traits: []
    notes: []
    created: 2026-03-08T08:01:50.912Z
    status:

  - _ulid: 01KK67F39FMSKW6S55K009Q60A
    slugs:

    title: Shell Injection Prevention
    type: constraint
    tags: []
    description: All subprocess spawning uses spawn() with argument arrays, never exec() with string

    status:
      implementation: implemented

1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from 'vitest'
     2→import { join } from 'path'
     3→
     4→// Mock electron app module before importing session-store
     5→vi.mock('electron', () => ({
     6→  app: {
     7→    getPath: vi.fn().mockReturnValue('/mock/userData')
     8→  }
     9→}))
    10→
    11→// Mock fs module
    12→vi.mock('fs', () => ({
    13→  existsSync: vi.fn(),
    14→  mkdirSync: vi.fn(),
    15→  readFileSync: vi.fn(),
    16→  writeFileSync: vi.fn()
    17→}))
    18→
    19→import { SessionStore, Workspace, Settings, WindowBounds } from '../session-store'
    20→import * as fs from 'fs'
    21→
    22→describe('SessionStore', () => {
    23→  const mockConfigDir = '/mock/userData/config'
    24→  const mockConfigPath = join(mockConfigDir, 'workspace.json')
    25→
    26→  beforeEach(() => {
    27→    vi.clearAllMocks()
    28→  })
    29→
    30→  afterEach(() => {
    31→    vi.restoreAllMocks()
    32→  })
    33→
    34→  describe('constructor', () => {
    35→    it('should create config directory if it does not exist', () => {
    36→      vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockReturnValueOnce(false) // config dir check
    37→      vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockReturnValueOnce(false) // workspace.json check
    38→
    39→      new SessionStore()
    40→
    41→      expect(fs.mkdirSync).toHaveBeenCalledWith(mockConfigDir, { recursive: true })
    42→    })
    43→
    44→    it('should not create config directory if it already exists', () => {
    45→      vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockReturnValueOnce(true) // config dir exists
    46→      vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockReturnValueOnce(false) // workspace.json doesn't exist
    47→
    48→      new SessionStore()
    49→
    50→      expect(fs.mkdirSync).not.toHaveBeenCalled()
    51→    })
    52→  })
    53→
    54→  describe('load()', () => {
    55→    it('should return default workspace when workspace.json does not exist', () => {
    56→      vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockReturnValueOnce(true) // config dir exists
    57→      vi.mocked(fs.existsSync).mockReturnValueOnce(false) // workspace.json doesn't exist
    58→
    59→      const store = new SessionStore()

In /home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal, find all files related to session discovery. The task description mentions: encoded path handling for special chars, OpenCode SQLite connection leaks, JSONL parsing for corrupted files, empty session filtering, race between discovery and active writes.

Search for:
1. Files with "session" and "discover" in their names or content
2. Files dealing with Claude ~/.claude/projects/ discovery
3. Files dealing with OpenCode .opencode/opencode.db SQLite
4. JSONL parsing related to sessions

- Current implementation details relevant to each bug area
- Specific code sections that might have the bugs mentioned

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report.

## Session Discovery - Bug Investigation Report

I've conducted a thorough search of the codebase and identified all files related to session discovery and the reported bugs. Here's my complete analysis:

### Key Files and Their Roles

#### 1. **Core Session Discovery Implementation**
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-discovery.ts`**
  - Main entry point for discovering sessions from Claude and OpenCode backends
  - Exports `discoverSessions(projectPath, backend)` async function
  - Functions: `discoverClaudeSessions()`, `discoverOpenCodeSessions()`, `encodeProjectPath()`, `loadOpenCodeConfig()`, `resolveOpenCodeDataDir()`

#### 2. **Session Store & Persistence**
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/session-store.ts`**
  - Manages workspace persistence in `~/.config/simple-code-gui/config/workspace.json`
  - Implements atomic writes with temp files and backup strategy
  - Recently fixed (commit 758a5a9) to clean up temp files and use atomic rename for backups

#### 3. **Meta-Project Symlink Management**
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/meta-project-sync.ts`**
  - Creates hierarchical symlink structure at `~/.config/simple-code-gui/meta-projects/`
  - Recently fixed (commit 758a5a9) to detect and remove **stale symlinks** pointing to deleted directories

#### 4. **IPC Handler Registration**
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/main/app/ipc-handlers/workspace.ts`**
  - Registers IPC handler: `ipcMain.handle('sessions:discover', ...)`
  - Bridges Electron renderer to session discovery

#### 5. **API Backends**
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/electron-backend.ts`**
  - Electron IPC wrapper for `discoverSessions()`
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/renderer/api/http-backend/workspace-api.ts`**
  - HTTP client implementation for remote servers

#### 6. **Server Routes**
- **`/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/src/server/routes/terminal.ts`**
  - REST endpoint: `GET /api/terminal/discover/:projectPath(*)`
  - Calls `services.discoverSessions(projectPath, backend)`

---

### Bug Analysis - Current Implementation Details

#### **Bug 1: Encoded Path Handling for Special Characters**

**Location:** `src/main/session-discovery.ts` lines 22-40

```typescript
function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
  return projectPath
    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
}
```

**Current Implementation Issues:**
- Aggressively encodes ALL special characters into dashes, making the output non-reversible and lossy
- Example: `/home/user/my_project` → `-home-user-my-project` (cannot be uniquely decoded)
- Example: `/home/user/.config/app` → `-home-user--config-app` (multiple dots collapse to single dash)
- No handling for Unicode characters or encoding edge cases
- The function replaces `.` with `-`, which means filenames like `my.project` become indistinguishable from `my-project`

**Location Used:** Line 85 when constructing path to `~/.claude/projects/`
- `const projectSessionsDir = join(claudeDir, encodedPath)`

---

#### **Bug 2: OpenCode SQLite Connection Leaks**

**Location:** `src/main/session-discovery.ts` lines 160-194

```typescript
function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {
  const dataDir = resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath)
  const dbPath = join(dataDir, 'opencode.db')

  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
    return []
  }

  try {
    const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
    const rows = db.prepare(`...`).all() as Array<...>
    db.close()  // Line 176
    
    return rows.filter(...).map(...)
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
    return []
  }
}
```

**Current Implementation Issues:**
- **Resource leak in success path:** If `db.close()` throws (unlikely but possible), connection stays open
- **Resource leak in catch path:** If `.prepare()` or `.all()` throws, `db.close()` is never called
- No try-finally block to guarantee resource cleanup
- The readonly flag mitigates some risk, but doesn't prevent lock contention or WAL file accumulation

**Better Pattern Needed:**
```typescript
try {
  const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
  try {
    const rows = db.prepare(...).all()
    return rows.filter(...).map(...)
  } finally {
    db.close()
  }
} catch (e) {
  console.error(...)
  return []
}
```

---

#### **Bug 3: JSONL Parsing for Corrupted Files**

**Location:** `src/main/session-discovery.ts` lines 107-126

```typescript
const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())  // Line 108

for (const line of lines) {
  try {
    const data = JSON.parse(line)  // Line 117
    if (data.slug) slug = data.slug
    if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
    if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {
      hasConversation = true
    }
  } catch {
    // Skip non-JSON lines  // Line 124
  }
}
```

**Current Implementation Issues:**
- **Silent failure on corrupted lines:** Corrupted JSON lines are silently skipped - the entire session may be lost due to missing metadata
- **Incomplete JSONL handling:** If the session file is truncated mid-record, the last incomplete line is silently dropped
- **No validation of parsed data:** No checks for required fields (slug, cwd, type) being present or valid
- **Multiple partial lines:** If file has trailing partial data, it's filtered by `.trim()` but no warning is logged
- **Character encoding issues:** No handling for invalid UTF-8 sequences that might appear in corrupted files

**Risk:** A partially-written or corrupted session file could lose all metadata (slug, cwd) and thus appear empty or improperly indexed.

---

#### **Bug 4: Empty Session Filtering**

**Location:** `src/main/session-discovery.ts` lines 128-130 (Claude) and line 179 (OpenCode)

**Claude Backend:**
```typescript
if (!hasConversation) {  // Line 128
  return null  // Filter out empty sessions
}
```

**OpenCode Backend:**
```typescript
return rows
  .filter(row => (row.message_count ?? 0) > 0)  // Line 179 - uses ?? operator
  .map(...)
```

**Current Implementation Issues:**
- **Inconsistent filtering logic:** Claude uses presence of CONVERSATION_TYPES (`user` or `assistant`), OpenCode uses `message_count > 0`
- **Different backends, different criteria:** A session with only system messages is filtered on Claude but might appear on OpenCode
- **Coalescing operator risk (OpenCode):** `row.message_count ?? 0` will return 0 if message_count is null/undefined - this is correct, but if the database returns falsy values like empty string `""`, the comparison `"" > 0` evaluates to `false` (correct by accident)
- **No session title fallback on Claude:** If neither `slug` nor conversation metadata exists, slug defaults to `sessionId.slice(0, 8)`, which might not be descriptive

---

#### **Bug 5: Race Between Discovery and Active Writes**

**Location:** Multiple files with no synchronization mechanism

**The Problem:**
1. **Session Discovery reads files:** `discoverSessions()` calls `readFile()` on `~/.claude/projects/sessionId.jsonl`
2. **Meanwhile, active session writes:** The running Claude session appends new lines to the same file
3. **Concurrent read-write on same file:**
   - JSONL is line-delimited, so partial writes mid-line can be corrupt
   - File stats (mtime, size) may be captured at inconsistent points
   - Linux/Unix file systems don't guarantee atomic partial-file reads

**Affected Code Paths:**

Claude Backend (lines 106-107):
```typescript
const fileStat = await stat(filePath)
const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
```

**Race Condition Sequence:**
1. Discovery calls `stat(filePath)` → captures mtime_A, size_A
2. Active session appends 100 bytes
3. Discovery calls `readFile(filePath)` → gets file with mtime_B, size_B but reads partial/inconsistent data
4. Active session appends another line (now file is mid-line corrupt)
5. Discovery parses - last line might be incomplete JSON

**OpenCode SQLite (line 169):**
```typescript
const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
```

**Race with SQLite:**
- While discovery reads, the OpenCode daemon might be writing to opencode.db and its WAL files
- SQLite handles read-write concurrency via WAL, but concurrent reads during active writes may get inconsistent snapshots
- If discovery captures an old snapshot of the database, it might not see recent sessions

**Session Store Persistence Race (session-store.ts):**
```typescript
// Line 190-197: Write to temp file, then rename
const tmpPath = this.configPath + '.tmp'
writeFileSync(tmpPath, json)
const written = readFileSync(tmpPath, 'utf-8')
JSON.parse(written)  // verify
renameSync(tmpPath, this.configPath)
```

**Recent Fix (commit 758a5a9):**
- Added cleanup of temp files on verification failure (line 204-206)
- Made backup writes atomic (temp+rename)
- But **no locking mechanism** between discovery and persistent writes

---

### Summary of Issues

| Bug | Severity | Impact | Status |
|-----|----------|--------|--------|
| Encoded path handling | Medium | Special chars in paths → wrong session lookup | Current implementation exists but is lossy |
| SQLite connection leaks | High | Resource exhaustion, lock contention | No try-finally guarantee |
| JSONL corrupted file parsing | Medium | Silent data loss on corruption | Silent failures with no logging |
| Empty session filtering | Low | Inconsistent filtering between backends | Different criteria per backend |
| Race condition (discovery vs writes) | High | Corrupted/incomplete session data | No synchronization or locking |

### Additional Context from Git

- **Most recent fix:** Commit `758a5a9` (2026-03-10) addressed workspace persistence, temp cleanup, and stale symlinks
- **Active task:** `.kspec/project.tasks.yaml` shows `@bug-discovery` task marked as `in_progress` since 2026-03-10
- **Spec reference:** Linked to `@session-discovery` module in `.kspec/modules/terminal.yaml` (feature marked as `in_progress`)

All file paths are absolute paths to the project root at `/home/user/Programs/Claude Projects/Claude-Terminal/`.

```typescript
  return projectPath
    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
}
```

**Current Implementation Issues:**

- Example: `/home/user/.config/app` → `-home-user--config-app` (multiple dots collapse to single dash)

```typescript
function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {

  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
    return []
  }

  try {
    const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
    const rows = db.prepare(`...`).all() as Array<...>
    db.close()  // Line 176
    
    return rows.filter(...).map(...)
  } catch (e) {
    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
    return []
  }
}
```

**Current Implementation Issues:**
- **Resource leak in success path:** If `db.close()` throws (unlikely but possible), connection stays open
- **Resource leak in catch path:** If `.prepare()` or `.all()` throws, `db.close()` is never called
- No try-finally block to guarantee resource cleanup
- The readonly flag mitigates some risk, but doesn't prevent lock contention or WAL file accumulation

```typescript

  const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })

    const rows = db.prepare(...).all()
    return rows.filter(...).map(...)
  } finally {
    db.close()

}
```

    19→const CONVERSATION_TYPES = ['user', 'assistant']
    20→const OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME = '.opencode.json'
    21→
    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
    23→  // Claude encodes paths by:
    24→  // 1. Removing trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replacing / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replacing _ with -
    27→  // 4. Replacing spaces with -
    28→  // 5. Replacing : with - (Windows drive letters)
    29→  // 6. Replacing dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  // /home/user/my_project/ becomes -home-user-my-project
    31→  // /home/user/.config/app becomes -home-user--config-app
    32→  // C:\Users\bob\project becomes -C-Users-bob-project
    33→  return projectPath
    34→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    35→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    36→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    37→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    38→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    39→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    40→}
    41→
    42→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    43→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    44→    return homedir()
    45→  }
    46→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    47→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    48→  }
    49→  return targetPath
    50→}
    51→
    52→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    53→  const home = homedir()
    54→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    55→  const candidatePaths = [
    56→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    57→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    58→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    59→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    60→  ]
    61→
    62→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    63→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    64→    try {
    65→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    66→      return JSON.parse(content)
    67→    } catch (e) {
    68→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    69→    }
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  return null
    73→}
    74→
    75→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    76→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
    77→  const configured = config?.data?.directory?.trim()
    78→  const dataDir = configured && configured.length > 0 ? configured : '.opencode'
    79→  const expanded = expandHomePath(dataDir)
    80→  return isAbsolute(expanded) ? expanded : join(projectPath, expanded)
    81→}
    82→
    83→async function discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath: string): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
    84→  const claudeDir = join(homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')
    85→  const encodedPath = encodeProjectPath(projectPath)
    86→  const projectSessionsDir = join(claudeDir, encodedPath)
    87→
    88→  if (!existsSync(projectSessionsDir)) {
    89→    return []
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  const sessions: DiscoveredSession[] = []
    93→
    94→  try {
    95→    const files = await readdir(projectSessionsDir)
    96→
    97→    // Process files in parallel for better performance
    98→    const results = await Promise.all(
    99→      files
   100→        .filter(file => file.endsWith('.jsonl') && !file.startsWith('agent-'))
   101→        .map(async (file) => {
   102→          const sessionId = file.replace('.jsonl', '')
   103→          const filePath = join(projectSessionsDir, file)
   104→
   105→          try {
   106→            const fileStat = await stat(filePath)
   107→            const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
   108→            const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())
   109→
   110→            // Look for slug and check if session has actual conversation
   111→            let slug = sessionId.slice(0, 8)
   112→            let cwd = projectPath
   113→            let hasConversation = false
   114→
   115→            for (const line of lines) {
   116→              try {
   117→                const data = JSON.parse(line)
   118→                if (data.slug) slug = data.slug
   119→                if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
   120→                if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {
   121→                  hasConversation = true
   122→                }
   123→              } catch {
   124→                // Skip non-JSON lines
   125→              }
   126→            }
   127→
   128→            if (!hasConversation) {
   129→              return null
   130→            }
   131→
   132→            return {
   133→              sessionId,
   134→              slug,
   135→              lastModified: fileStat.mtimeMs,
   136→              cwd,
   137→              fileSize: fileStat.size
   138→            }
   139→          } catch (e) {
   140→            console.error(`Failed to parse session ${file}:`, e)
   141→            return null
   142→          }
   143→        })
   144→    )
   145→
   146→    // Filter out nulls and add to sessions
   147→    for (const result of results) {
   148→      if (result) sessions.push(result)
   149→    }
   150→  } catch (e) {
   151→    console.error('Failed to read sessions directory:', e)
   152→  }
   153→
   154→  // Sort by most recent first
   155→  sessions.sort((a, b) => b.lastModified - a.lastModified)
   156→
   157→  return sessions
   158→}
   159→
   160→function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {
   161→  const dataDir = resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath)
   162→  const dbPath = join(dataDir, 'opencode.db')
   163→
   164→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
   165→    return []
   166→  }
   167→
   168→  try {
   169→    const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
   170→    const rows = db.prepare(`
   171→      SELECT id, title, updated_at, created_at, message_count
   172→      FROM sessions
   173→      WHERE parent_session_id IS NULL
   174→      ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   175→    `).all() as Array<{ id: string; title: string | null; updated_at: number; created_at: number; message_count: number }>
   176→    db.close()
   177→
   178→    return rows
   179→      .filter(row => (row.message_count ?? 0) > 0)
   180→      .map(row => {
   181→        const lastModifiedSeconds = row.updated_at || row.created_at || 0
   182→        return {
   183→          sessionId: row.id,
   184→          slug: (row.title && row.title.trim()) || row.id.slice(0, 8),
   185→          lastModified: Number(lastModifiedSeconds) * 1000,
   186→          cwd: projectPath,
   187→          fileSize: 0
   188→        }
   189→      })
   190→  } catch (e) {
   191→    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
   192→    return []
   193→  }
   194→}
   195→
   196→export async function discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' = 'claude'): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
   197→  if (backend === 'opencode') {
   198→    return discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath)
   199→  }
   200→  return discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath)
   201→}
   202→

```
1→import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'fs'
     2→import { readdir, readFile, stat } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join, isAbsolute } from 'path'
     4→import { homedir } from 'os'
     5→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-var-requires
     6→const Database = require('better-sqlite3')
     7→
     8→export interface DiscoveredSession {
     9→  sessionId: string
    10→  slug: string
    11→  lastModified: number
    12→  cwd: string
    13→  fileSize: number
    14→}
    15→
    16→export type SessionBackend = 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider'
    17→
    18→// Message types that indicate actual conversation content (not just summaries)
    19→const CONVERSATION_TYPES = ['user', 'assistant']
    20→const OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME = '.opencode.json'
    21→
    22→function encodeProjectPath(projectPath: string): string {
```
    23→  // Claude's current encoding scheme:
    24→  // 1. Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    25→  // 2. Replace / and \ with -
    26→  // 3. Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    27→  // 4. Replace _ with -
    28→  // 5. Replace spaces with -
    29→  // 6. Replace dots with dashes (e.g. .config -> -config)
    30→  return projectPath
    31→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')  // Remove trailing slashes/backslashes
    32→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')  // Replace / and \ with -
    33→    .replace(/:/g, '-')      // Replace : with - (Windows drive letters)
    34→    .replace(/_/g, '-')      // Replace _ with -
    35→    .replace(/ /g, '-')      // Replace spaces with -
    36→    .replace(/\./g, '-')     // Replace dots with dashes
    37→}
    38→
    39→// Claude used a different encoding in older versions (no leading dash, spaces preserved)
    40→function encodeProjectPathLegacy(projectPath: string): string {
    41→  return projectPath
    42→    .replace(/[/\\]+$/, '')
    43→    .replace(/^[/\\]/, '')   // Strip leading slash (no leading dash)
    44→    .replace(/[/\\]/g, '-')
    45→    .replace(/:/g, '-')
    46→    .replace(/_/g, '-')
    47→    .replace(/\./g, '-')
    48→    // Spaces are NOT replaced in the legacy format
    49→}
    50→
    51→function expandHomePath(targetPath: string): string {
    52→  if (targetPath === '~') {
    53→    return homedir()
    54→  }
    55→  if (targetPath.startsWith('~/') || targetPath.startsWith('~\\')) {
    56→    return join(homedir(), targetPath.slice(2))
    57→  }
    58→  return targetPath
    59→}
    60→
    61→function loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath: string): { data?: { directory?: string } } | null {
    62→  const home = homedir()
    63→  const xdgConfig = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME
    64→  const candidatePaths = [
    65→    join(projectPath, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    66→    ...(xdgConfig ? [join(xdgConfig, 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)] : []),
    67→    join(home, '.config', 'opencode', OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME),
    68→    join(home, OPENCODE_CONFIG_NAME)
    69→  ]
    70→
    71→  for (const configPath of candidatePaths) {
    72→    if (!existsSync(configPath)) continue
    73→    try {
    74→      const content = readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')
    75→      return JSON.parse(content)
    76→    } catch (e) {
    77→      console.error('Failed to parse OpenCode config:', e)
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  return null
    82→}
    83→
    84→function resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath: string): string {
    85→  const config = loadOpenCodeConfig(projectPath)
    86→  const configured = config?.data?.directory?.trim()
    87→  const dataDir = configured && configured.length > 0 ? configured : '.opencode'
    88→  const expanded = expandHomePath(dataDir)
    89→  return isAbsolute(expanded) ? expanded : join(projectPath, expanded)
    90→}
    91→
    92→async function discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath: string): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
    93→  const claudeDir = join(homedir(), '.claude', 'projects')
    94→
    95→  // Check both current and legacy encoding schemes to find all sessions
    96→  const candidateDirs = [
    97→    join(claudeDir, encodeProjectPath(projectPath)),
    98→    join(claudeDir, encodeProjectPathLegacy(projectPath))
    99→  ]
   100→  // Deduplicate (they may produce the same result for paths without spaces)
   101→  const uniqueDirs = [...new Set(candidateDirs)].filter(dir => existsSync(dir))
   102→
   103→  if (uniqueDirs.length === 0) {
   104→    return []
   105→  }
   106→
   107→  const sessions = new Map<string, DiscoveredSession>()
   108→
   109→  for (const projectSessionsDir of uniqueDirs) {
   110→    try {
   111→      const files = await readdir(projectSessionsDir)
   112→
   113→      // Process files in parallel for better performance
   114→      const results = await Promise.all(
   115→        files
   116→          .filter(file => file.endsWith('.jsonl') && !file.startsWith('agent-'))
   117→          .map(async (file) => {
   118→            const sessionId = file.replace('.jsonl', '')
   119→            const filePath = join(projectSessionsDir, file)
   120→
   121→            try {
   122→              // Read content first, then stat — ensures mtime/size reflect
   123→              // at least the content we read (file may grow from active writes)
   124→              const content = await readFile(filePath, 'utf-8')
   125→              const fileStat = await stat(filePath)
   126→              const lines = content.split('\n').filter(line => line.trim())
   127→
   128→              // Look for slug and check if session has actual conversation
   129→              let slug = sessionId.slice(0, 8)
   130→              let cwd = projectPath
   131→              let hasConversation = false
   132→              let parseErrors = 0
   133→
   134→              for (const line of lines) {
   135→                try {
   136→                  const data = JSON.parse(line)
   137→                  if (data.slug) slug = data.slug
   138→                  if (data.cwd) cwd = data.cwd
   139→                  if (data.type && CONVERSATION_TYPES.includes(data.type)) {
   140→                    hasConversation = true
   141→                  }
   142→                } catch {
   143→                  parseErrors++
   144→                }
   145→              }
   146→
   147→              // Log if many lines failed to parse (suggests file corruption,
   148→              // not just a single truncated line from a concurrent write)
   149→              if (parseErrors > 1) {
   150→                console.warn(`Session ${file}: ${parseErrors}/${lines.length} lines failed to parse`)
   151→              }
   152→
   153→              if (!hasConversation) {
   154→                return null
   155→              }
   156→
   157→              return {
   158→                sessionId,
   159→                slug,
   160→                lastModified: fileStat.mtimeMs,
   161→                cwd,
   162→                fileSize: fileStat.size
   163→              }
   164→            } catch (e) {
   165→              console.error(`Failed to parse session ${file}:`, e)
   166→              return null
   167→            }
   168→          })
   169→      )
   170→
   171→      // Deduplicate by sessionId (same session may appear in both dirs)
   172→      for (const result of results) {
   173→        if (result && !sessions.has(result.sessionId)) {
   174→          sessions.set(result.sessionId, result)
   175→        }
   176→      }
   177→    } catch (e) {
   178→      console.error('Failed to read sessions directory:', e)
   179→    }
   180→  }
   181→
   182→  // Sort by most recent first
   183→  const sorted = [...sessions.values()]
   184→  sorted.sort((a, b) => b.lastModified - a.lastModified)
   185→
   186→  return sorted
   187→}
   188→
   189→function discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath: string): DiscoveredSession[] {
   190→  const dataDir = resolveOpenCodeDataDir(projectPath)
   191→  const dbPath = join(dataDir, 'opencode.db')
   192→
   193→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
   194→    return []
   195→  }
   196→
   197→  let db: InstanceType<typeof Database> | null = null
   198→  try {
   199→    db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true })
   200→    const rows = db.prepare(`
   201→      SELECT id, title, updated_at, created_at, message_count
   202→      FROM sessions
   203→      WHERE parent_session_id IS NULL
   204→      ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   205→    `).all() as Array<{ id: string; title: string | null; updated_at: number; created_at: number; message_count: number }>
   206→
   207→    return rows
   208→      .filter(row => (row.message_count ?? 0) > 0)
   209→      .map(row => {
   210→        const lastModifiedSeconds = row.updated_at || row.created_at || 0
   211→        return {
   212→          sessionId: row.id,
   213→          slug: (row.title && row.title.trim()) || row.id.slice(0, 8),
   214→          lastModified: Number(lastModifiedSeconds) * 1000,
   215→          cwd: projectPath,
   216→          fileSize: 0
   217→        }
   218→      })
   219→  } catch (e) {
   220→    console.error('Failed to read OpenCode sessions:', e)
   221→    return []
   222→  } finally {
   223→    try { db?.close() } catch { /* already errored */ }
   224→  }
   225→}
   226→
   227→export async function discoverSessions(projectPath: string, backend: 'claude' | 'gemini' | 'codex' | 'opencode' | 'aider' = 'claude'): Promise<DiscoveredSession[]> {
   228→  if (backend === 'opencode') {
   229→    return discoverOpenCodeSessions(projectPath)
   230→  }
   231→  return discoverClaudeSessions(projectPath)
   232→}
   233→
